message:
fix(migrations): set restrictive file permissions (0o600) on migrated auth JSON

The auth-to-auth.json migration writes credentials to a temporary file
before renaming. Previously it used fs::File::create() which inherits
the default umask, potentially creating the file as world-readable
(0o644). On multi-user systems this leaks API keys and tokens.

Now uses OpenOptions with explicit mode(0o600) on Unix to ensure only
the owning user can read the migrated credentials file. The permission
restriction is cfg-gated to Unix only; Windows uses its own ACL model.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
